codeReview.summary: Core Clerk wiring (middleware, provider, auth pages, and header states) was implemented, but the user-sync behavior is not wired into any runtime path, so first-login DB user creation cannot occur.

issues.description: The feature requires syncing Clerk users into the DB on protected page loads, but `syncUser` is never invoked anywhere in runtime code. In this commit, `syncUser` appears only in its own module and test file (`git grep "syncUser" <commit>`), so successful login does not create/update a `users` row.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker procedure says to write failing tests before implementation, but this transcript shows implementation files were created/edited before the test file. Either enforcement is weak or the procedure should clarify when strict TDD is required.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl (session e3c1a44f-4002-45d3-834e-dd067b03bda1): Create/Edit of src/middleware.ts, auth pages, sync-user.ts, header.tsx, layout.tsx occur before Create src/actions/sync-user.test.ts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Clerk v7 API nuance (using `Show` instead of `SignedIn`/`SignedOut`) was discovered during implementation but is not captured in shared library docs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges explicitly asks to document that @clerk/nextjs v7 no longer exports SignedIn/SignedOut; .factory/library/environment.md currently does not mention this.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking correctness gap: user sync is implemented but never called, so DB user creation on first authenticated access is not satisfied. Added two shared-state observations about skill/procedure clarity and missing Clerk v7 knowledge capture.
